title: NaN should be avoided in comparisons
description: "NaN" should not be used in comparisonsAnalyze your codeintentionality-logicalreliabilityBugWhy is this an issue?How can I fix it?double.NaNandfloat.NaNare not equal to anything, not even themselves.When anything is compared withNaNusing one of thecomparison operators>,>=,<,⇐or theequality operator==, the result will always befalse. In contrast, when anything is compared withNaNusing theinequality operator!=, the result will always betrue.Instead, the best way to see whether a variable is equal toNaNis to use thefloat.IsNaNanddouble.IsNaNmethods, which work as expected.Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab repositoriesAnalyze code in youron-premise CI©2025SonarSource Sàrl. All rights reserved.Privacy Pol
recommendation: Replace with the recommended alternative approach.

checks.description: Check for direct comparisons with NaN values that should use IsNaN() instead
